situationLines: The morning light comes in low and gold over the rails, and you are the only one who hears the trolley coming.
Down the main line, five backs bent to their work, sleeves rolled, a radio playing somewhere none of them can hear.
Your hand rests on the lever, and the spur curves off to where one man stands alone, looking the other way.
stakes: five strangers at their work · one man who never turns around

choices.label: keep your hands at your sides
choices.consequence: The trolley stays its course and the five on the main line go under it.

voices.count: Four more than one went still on the rail this morning, and the larger number is the one you let fall.
voices.faith: You bent no rail toward anyone, and your palms are cool and empty while five sets of lungs go quiet.
voices.tend: Five radios kept playing to no one, and you knew the shape of every back you watched go down.
voices.agree: Lying blind on either track, would they have wanted a world where the hand on the lever simply stays cool?
voices.virtue: You are someone who will not steer death, and you will carry the five you steered nowhere.

choices.label: throw the lever
choices.consequence: The trolley swings onto the spur and the lone man goes under it.

voices.count: One body bought four breathing ones, and the arithmetic holds even as it turns your stomach.
voices.faith: You sent the wheels toward a man who was only standing there, and your hand still remembers the throw.
voices.tend: He never turned around, and now you are the only one who will keep the face he never showed you.
voices.agree: Blind to which track they'd wake on, would they have wanted the rule that spends the lonely one?
voices.virtue: You are someone who moves to save, and you will live as the kind who moved a stranger into the wheels to do it.

situationLines: The footbridge hums under your feet as the trolley comes on fast below.
Beside you a large stranger leans on the rail, watching, unaware his weight is the only thing heavy enough to stop it.
Five stand on the track downstream, backs turned, too far to hear you scream.
stakes: five strangers downstream · one warm shoulder at your elbow

choices.label: keep your hands at your sides
choices.consequence: Your hands stay on the cold rail and the trolley takes the five below.

voices.count: Four more went down than had to, and you stood here counting them as they fell.
voices.faith: Your palms never met his back, and five sets of lungs stopped while yours stayed clean.
voices.tend: The stranger's shoulder is still warm beside you, and five faces you let the trolley find are going cold.
voices.agree: Blind to which track they'd wake on, would they have wanted a world where no back is ever shoved, even this one?
voices.virtue: You kept yourself from becoming the kind of hands that push, and the five paid for the person you would not be.

choices.label: press him over the rail
choices.consequence: He goes over at your hands, his body jams the rail, and the five walk away never knowing.

voices.count: One body for five breathing on, and the one was breathing too when you decided it.
voices.faith: You crossed the line you swore you never would, and four strangers will wake tomorrow because you did.
voices.tend: His weight still hangs in your palms, the warmth of a shoulder you turned into a brake, a man you stood beside and used.
voices.agree: Blind to which rail they'd wake on, all six might have wanted this, and the one you pushed never got to say.
voices.virtue: You made yourself someone who can spend a man like a coin, and five strangers owe their morning to that exact hand.

situationLines: The lever is cool under your palm, the same dull iron as before.
But this track bends back on itself, a loop that rejoins the line past the five — and only the weight of the one man standing on it would ever stop the wheels.
Divert, and the trolley does not miss them; his body is the brake.
stakes: five strangers on the long straight · one man whose body the iron needs

choices.label: keep your hands at your sides
choices.consequence: The trolley holds the main line and the five are struck where they stand.

voices.count: Five gone and one still standing, and the number ran the way you would not turn it.
voices.faith: The one on the loop is breathing because you never made him into a stopper, and that breath cost five others theirs.
voices.tend: Five faces you could have spared, and you stood close enough to watch each of them turn.
voices.agree: Blind to which track they'd wake on, would five of them have wanted a rule that lets your hands stay still?
voices.virtue: You kept yourself from using him, and in the keeping you became someone who could watch five and not move.

choices.label: throw the lever (onto the loop)
choices.consequence: The trolley rounds the loop, his body halts the wheels, and the five walk away.

voices.count: Four still standing because of you, and one you spent on purpose, knowing the loop needed him whole.
voices.faith: You did not just steer the iron; you reached for him, made him the thing that stops it, and your hand knew it.
voices.tend: He stood alone on the bend with a name you never asked, and you chose his body to be the wall.
voices.agree: Blind to which track they'd wake on, would anyone have wanted to be the one the wheels are aimed through?
voices.virtue: You saved four, and you became the kind of person who can look at a man and see a brake.

situationLines: Five strangers stand on the main line, backs turned, talking about nothing.
On the side spur, alone, is the face you would know in the dark — already lifting a hand to wave at you.
The lever is cold under your palm and the car is already coming.
stakes: five strangers you'll never name · the one you'd carry out of any fire

choices.label: keep your hands at your sides
choices.consequence: The car runs straight through the five and your beloved walks home unharmed.

voices.count: Four more went down so one could keep waving at you, and the number sits in you like a stone.
voices.faith: Your hands never moved, and five people you owed nothing to paid for that stillness.
voices.tend: You kept the one face you couldn't lose, and somewhere five tables are set for no one.
voices.agree: Blind to which body they'd wear, would they have wanted a world where your love outweighs their five?
voices.virtue: You found out you are someone who would let the world go dark for one warm hand, and you will carry that knowing.

choices.label: throw the lever
choices.consequence: The car swings onto the spur and the one you love is gone where five strangers now stand breathing.

voices.count: Five hearts still beating against one, and the arithmetic is plain and it is the cruelest thing your hands have done.
voices.faith: You turned the one hand you'd have died for into the thing that stopped the car, and no number lifts it off your palm.
voices.tend: The last thing they did was wave at you, and you sent the weight straight at them and watched.
voices.agree: Blind to which body they'd wear, every stranger on that track might have wanted this, and your love was never asked.
voices.virtue: You became someone who could spend the dearest life for strangers, and you will live the rest of yours beside that face.

situationLines: Five beds. Five monitors counting down in the blue dark, each one waiting for a part it will not get by morning.
Down the hall, a healthy stranger reads a magazine in the waiting room, warm and breathing and whole.
Inside that one body is everything the five need; your hands know exactly where each piece would go.
stakes: five strangers in the beds · one stranger in the chair

choices.label: let the five slip away
choices.consequence: You leave the waiting room untouched, and by morning five monitors have gone flat.

voices.count: Five emptied beds against one warm chair, and the number sits cold in your chest.
voices.faith: Your hands stayed where they were, and five sets of breathing slowed to nothing while they did.
voices.tend: You knew their faces from rounds, and you let each one go quiet without going in.
voices.agree: Blind to which room they'd wake in, no one would have wanted to be opened, and no one would have wanted to be left.
voices.virtue: You are the kind who will not reach into a stranger, and five families learn tonight what that holds back from them.

choices.label: take what the one carries
choices.consequence: You bring the stranger in, and by morning five monitors hold steady while one goes still.

voices.count: One body parceled into five, and the number comes out even as your hands won't.
voices.faith: You crossed the one line you swore you never would, and five rooms are loud with living because you did.
voices.tend: You walked past the magazine still open on the chair, past the face that smiled at you in the hall.
voices.agree: Blind to which bed they'd wake in, no one would have wanted this, and yet five of them woke.
voices.virtue: You became someone who can do this, and five people will never know whose nerve bought their morning.

situationLines: The wheel turns itself under your loose hands, and the brake pedal sinks to the floor with nothing behind it.
Three shapes step into the crosswalk ahead, lit gold, not yet looking up.
To the right, a concrete pillar slides toward you; you are the only body this car holds.
stakes: three strangers in the headlights · the one warm seat, which is yours

choices.label: let it hold the lane
choices.consequence: The car keeps its line and the three are struck where they stand.

voices.count: Three for one, and the number ran against them even as it kept you breathing.
voices.faith: Your hands stayed on your own life and never shoved theirs aside, and three people paid for keeping them clean.
voices.tend: You saw the gold light on three faces in the second before, and you kept your own warm seat instead.
voices.agree: Blind to which seat they'd wake in, no one would have wanted a car that spends three for the one inside, yet here you still are.
voices.virtue: You became someone who outlives the people in front of the glass, and you will carry those faces into every mirror.

choices.label: turn it into the wall
choices.consequence: The car cuts right into the pillar and the seat you are sitting in takes the wall.

voices.count: One against three, and you made yourself the smaller number, the only one that was ever yours to give.
voices.faith: You reached past your own body and fed it to the wall, and the three who walk away will never know what your hands did to you.
voices.tend: Three strangers keep their faces and their names, and the last warm thing in the car was you.
voices.agree: Blind to which seat they'd wake in, anyone might have wanted this car that spares the crowd, and still it was you who woke holding the cost.
voices.virtue: You became the kind of person who steps in front, and the wall does not care what it makes of you.
